ops.title: getaway now batches CHANGELOG entries under [Unreleased] without bumping plugin.json per feature

ops.body:
As of 2026-07-11 (commit 53f32bc), getaway's changelog practice changed: new work is appended under `## [Unreleased]` and plugin.json's version stays put until the owner cuts a release. Earlier commits bumped plugin.json and cut a dated entry per feature — don't revert to that.

**Why:** the owner left plugin.json at 0.6.0 while landing substantive features under [Unreleased]; a per-feature bump would release someone else's pending work.

**How to apply:** when landing a change, append to the existing [Unreleased] Added/Changed lists and leave `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` untouched.

**Cache nuance (2026-07-13):** the installed-plugin cache is keyed by version (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/getaway/getaway/<version>/`), and `claude plugin update` no-ops at an unchanged version even after `plugin marketplace update` pulls the new commits — so batched [Unreleased] work does NOT reach the installed plugin until a release is cut (rename [Unreleased] → `[X.Y.Z] - date`, bump plugin.json, ship, then `claude plugin update`). If new preference-schema keys land in `~/.getaway/preferences.json` while the cache still runs the old `getaway.sh`, every cached `prefs-set` exits 3 (merged file carries non-template keys) — seed new-schema data only after the cache is on the new version.
